dialogueChunks.intro: I found something in my master's workshop that I wish I hadn't. Ledger entries that don't add up. Payments to people who shouldn't be receiving them.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My master taught me everything I know. I trusted them completely. But {quest_focus} shows years of skimming from apprentice fees, falsifying material costs, and pocketing the difference. Every student who paid for training was cheated. Every piece of work delivered to a client used inferior materials at premium prices.
dialogueChunks.outro: I need someone from outside the guild to {quest_action}. If I'm wrong, I've slandered the person I respect most. If I'm right, {quest_threat} is worse than anyone imagines. Either way, I can't look away. Not anymore.

playerResponses.text: Truth is truth, even when it hurts. I'll look into it.
Are you sure you want this door opened? It doesn't close again.
Internal guild matters are best handled internally.

dialogueChunks.intro: Every child in this settlement grows up hearing about the Battle of Thornfield and the hero who saved us. I just learned that story is a lie.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The 'hero' didn't rally the defenders. {quest_focus} is that they ran at the first charge and hid in a cellar while others fought and died. They came out afterward, covered in someone else's blood, and claimed the victory. The real defenders were dead and couldn't contradict the story. Now that person sits on the council, revered and powerful.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_origin} still has evidence: weapon positions, barricade placement, things that don't match the official story. Someone needs to {quest_action} before this person's false reputation lets them make decisions that cost more lives.

playerResponses.text: A hero built on lies is a danger to everyone who trusts them.
The settlement might be better off not knowing.
Old battles and old lies aren't my concern.

dialogueChunks.intro: The relief fund was supposed to help families displaced by the {enemy_type} raids. Three hundred contributions from across the region. The fund is empty.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The administrator: the most respected, trusted, charitable person in the settlement: has been diverting donations to their own accounts. {quest_focus} is staggering in its scale. {quest_stakes} who were promised shelter and supplies received nothing. The administrator still gives public speeches about compassion.
dialogueChunks.outro: The records are scattered across multiple locations to avoid detection. Someone needs to {quest_action} and build a case that can't be denied or buried. {quest_threat} has enough influence to silence anyone from within the community.

playerResponses.text: Stealing from refugees is as low as it gets. I'll find the evidence.
How deep does this person's influence go?
